matching_rule: One base case matches when a promoter or gene-body variant is visible in the current sample. Three additional case families match when the whitelist mean beta, the gene-name-related mean beta, or the all-numeric mean beta resolves to low, medium, or high methylation. Variant prediction rules receive the decoded sample GT, zygosity, ALT dosage, and site REF -> ALT definition; curated allele rules take precedence only when the sample genotype carries the relevant allele dosage.
disclaimer: Predictive theses in this database are literature-guided research summaries derived from the bundled gene interpretation bundle. They are designed for exploratory synthesis in the UI and should not be treated as diagnostic or therapeutic claims.
concrete_variant_prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests a 17q12-q21 asthma-locus thesis: the individual may carry a regulatory haplotype relevant to ORMDL3, GSDMB, sphingolipid homeostasis, childhood asthma, or allergic airway inflammation. This is locus-level context rather than a single-gene causal assignment.

variant_prediction_rules.prediction: GT-confirmed rs7216389 dosage suggests this sample carries the ORMDL3 prediction context described by the gene-level thesis. The variant observed in this sample suggests a 17q12-q21 asthma-locus thesis: the individual may carry a regulatory haplotype relevant to ORMDL3, GSDMB, sphingolipid homeostasis, childhood asthma, or allergic airway inflammation. This is locus-level context rather than a single-gene causal assignment. This specific marker is curated as 17q12-q21 ORMDL3/GSDMB asthma haplotype marker. rs7216389 is bundled as a ORMDL3 research marker for allergic disease, asthma, eczema, airway inflammation, or immune-regulatory cohort literature. Treat it as low-penetrance, cohort-aware association context rather than as a diagnostic or treatment-selection allele.
variant_prediction_rules.sample_change_template: This sample matched rs7216389 at site definition {change} with GT {gt_raw} decoded as {genotype} ({zygosity}). Anchor the ORMDL3 prediction to genotype dosage {allele_dosage} rather than REF -> ALT presence alone. Dosage-specific conclusions should be softened when GT, depth, balance, or genotype quality are uncertain.
variant_prediction_rules.basis: May tag regulatory, expression, coding, haplotype, or linked-locus variation around ORMDL3.

cases.label: Gene variant found
cases.prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests a 17q12-q21 asthma-locus thesis: the individual may carry a regulatory haplotype relevant to ORMDL3, GSDMB, sphingolipid homeostasis, childhood asthma, or allergic airway inflammation. This is locus-level context rather than a single-gene causal assignment. An observed ORMDL3 variant places this sample in the curated ORMDL3 research context. The bundled ORMDL3 knowledge base is focused on asthma, allergy, atopic dermatitis, airway inflammation, epithelial-barrier biology, and immune-regulatory research. Seeded markers and locus models are intended as research context and should not be promoted to diagnostic, prognostic, or treatment-selection claims without external clinical curation. ORMDL3 locus markers often reflect 17q12-q21 regulatory haplotypes that influence ORMDL3, GSDMB, and neighboring gene expression.
cases.rationale: This is the base thesis that activates as soon as any promoter or gene-body variant is visible in the current sample.

cases.label: Gene variant found + high whitelist mean beta
cases.prediction: When a ORMDL3 variant is paired with high methylation in the whitelist mean beta, the sample best fits a combined regulatory-context thesis. This pattern leans toward stronger regulatory restraint or reduced local accessibility around the locus. The variant side of the thesis is: The variant observed in this sample suggests a 17q12-q21 asthma-locus thesis: the individual may carry a regulatory haplotype relevant to ORMDL3, GSDMB, sphingolipid homeostasis, childhood asthma, or allergic airway inflammation. This is locus-level context rather than a single-gene causal assignment. ORMDL3 methylation should be read as local regulatory context around an allergic-inflammation gene.
